description: Repercussions of Cuban Crisis Being Felt in Europe By BERNARD KAPLAN PARIS NANA The back wash of the Cuban crisis is only now really reaching Western Europe but it has produced strong and surprising tidal cur rents along these shores For an untoward result of the Cuban affair has been to gen erate a new toughness in Ame ricas attitude toward its Europ ean allies Within the last few days evi dence has accumulated that in spired by the selfconfidence furnished by events in the Ca ribbean the United States has embarked on a decidedly more determined course in its chief tainship of the Western Alli ance A senior NATO official put it more bluntly when he said privately that Americai has decided to crack the whip The first target of the whip is clear It is to compel the European Allies to assume a far larger share of the defense of their continent to do so quickly and to sacrifice whatever narrow national interests must be sa crilced in order to do o BARGED STATEMENTS This is the wider meaning be hind the US decision no long er to disguise its belief that the British nuclear VBomber force is
